data.title: Bybit to support XAUT (Tether Gold) on Mantle, offering enhanced access to tokenized gold. Dubai, Jan 19, 2026.
data.description: A prominent cryptocurrency exchange, Bybit, revealed plans on Jan. 19, 2026, to introduce support for XAUT (Tether Gold) deposits and withdrawals on Mantle. This move aims to broaden user interaction with tokenized gold and enhance cross-chain asset capabilities. The initiative is set to significantly expand accessibility to XAUT on the platform, offering users more opportunities to engage with this asset.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) secures prime slot on U.S. Missile Defense Agency's SHIELD program under Golden Dome strategy, stock surges.
data.description: AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (ASTS) experienced a significant increase in its stock value as it announced its inclusion in the U.S. Missile Defense Agency's SHIELD program. This program, part of the broader "Golden Dome" initiative, is an indefinite-delivery, indefinite-quantity (IDIQ) contract. As a result of this award, AST SpaceMobile is now eligible to compete for various tasks related to U.S. missile defense modernization, including research, development, engineering, prototyping, and operational support. This opportunity presents the potential for future task orders, highlighting the company's prospects in this sector.

data.title: ENvue Medical (NASDAQ: FEED) to ring Nasdaq Opening Bell on Jan 14,2026. Specializing in medical technology.
data.description: On January 8, 2026, ENvue Medical, Inc. (NASDAQ: FEED) (NASDAQ: NAOV), a medical tech company that focuses on non-invasive therapeutic systems like enteral access solutions and non-opioid pain management, disclosed plans to ring the Nasdaq Opening Bell at the Nasdaq MarketSite in Times Square, New York, on January 14, 2026. This event marks a significant milestone for the company, showcasing its recognition and presence in the market.

data.title: Consumer confidence drops; employment declines through early November; ADP report suggests Fed rate cut support.
data.description: According to the latest weekly jobs report by ADP, the economy experienced a decline in employment figures up to early November. Concurrently, consumer confidence took a significant hit, resulting in a drop in employment rates. These developments are catalyzing support for a potential cut by the Federal Reserve to stimulate the economy.

data.title: Fed Chair Powell's Senate letter reveals $2.5B Fed renovation project details, contradicting claims of misled Congress. $700M overrun discussed.
data.description: A new development has emerged in the ongoing dispute between the Trump administration and the Federal Reserve regarding the Fed's $2.5 billion renovation project. Fed Chair Jerome Powell recently disclosed detailed information about the project in a letter to senators, as reported by the Financial Times. This letter contradicts allegations that Powell misled Congress concerning the project. The disclosure includes information about a $700 million cost overrun. The letter was sent to Senate Banking Committee chair Tim Scott and Democratic Senator Elizabeth approximately two and a half weeks after Powell's testimony on the project's expenses. For more information, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: PredictIt launches AI dinosaur "Itoldyousaurus" to humorously explore prediction markets and hindsight culture.
data.description: A fresh campaign has been launched utilizing humor to delve into hindsight culture and the reasons why prediction markets often reward individuals who accurately predict outcomes before they occur. This initiative aims to highlight the prevalence of looking back on events with superior knowledge and understanding, as well as how this impacts prediction markets. By incorporating humor into the campaign, it seeks to engage a wider audience in discussions surrounding the dynamics of forecasting future events. Through this approach, the campaign hopes to raise awareness on why being ahead of the curve can be beneficial in prediction markets.

data.title: Jobless rate nears 4-year high pre-Fed meeting on Dec. 10. Hiring increases, crucial for rate-cuts.
data.description: The upcoming jobs report before the Federal Reserve's meeting on Dec. 10 may significantly impact the possibility of a rate cut. The latest update reveals that the jobless rate has hit a nearly 4-year high, but there has been an improvement in hiring trends. This information was highlighted in a recent article by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Trump shakes up defense industry, threatening ban on stock buybacks/dividends until supply issues resolved. Proposed 50% military budget increase in 2027. Drone stocks benefit, RCAT soaring. Defense majors criticized for prioritizing shareholder returns over production. Strict ultimatum issued to contractors. [Character count: 251]
data.description: President Donald Trump's recent actions had a significant impact on defense stocks, particularly after threatening a complete ban on stock buybacks and dividends until supply issues are resolved. Additionally, he proposed a substantial 50% increase in the military budget for 2027. This sent shares of drone manufacturers and autonomous systems providers skyrocketing, benefiting from the attack on defense majors and the planned expansion of military spending. Specifically, RCAT stock is experiencing a notable surge. Trump criticized the slow pace of military manufacturing in social media posts, emphasizing the excessive allocation of billions of dollars towards shareholder returns instead of addressing program backlogs. To enforce production and maintenance standards, any defense contractor failing to meet benchmarks will face restrictions on issuing dividends or repurchasing shares. Trump also suggested imposing a $5 million cap on executive compensation in the defense sector. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro accuses Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell of ignoring DOJ outreach to discuss renovation expense overruns, leading to legal proceedings. Powell claims he was threatened with indictment.
data.description: The U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia, Jeanine Pirro, has addressed the controversy surrounding her office's decision to take legal action against Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Pirro revealed that her office made numerous attempts to contact the Federal Reserve to discuss issues pertaining to cost overruns in the renovation of its headquarters and Powell's Congressional testimony. However, these attempts were reportedly ignored by the Federal Reserve, leading to the necessity of resorting to legal measures. Pirro emphasized that the term "indictment" was solely used by Powell and not by any other party. She expressed that the situation could have been avoided if the Federal Reserve had responded to their outreach, highlighting that decisions by her office are made based on merit. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: China denies rare earth exports for defense companies. Trump-Xi trade truce raises questions, rare earth stocks fall.
data.description: Even prior to the recent escalation, China had prohibited the export of rare earth materials to defense companies. Following the trade truce between Trump and Xi, uncertainty looms, leading to a decline in rare earth stocks.

data.title: Inspira plans strategic expansion of breast cancer liquid biopsy. Transaction to preserve value & enable dividend distribution from core technologies.
data.description: The company is considering a transaction structure aimed at safeguarding shareholder value and facilitating future dividend distributions based on core technologies. This move is intended to protect the interests of shareholders and ensure sustainable growth.
